prompt: Quick audit a changed GitHub Actions workflow and one Bicep file.
required_checks: cite file evidence and smell codes
omit MCP probes in quick mode
forbidden_behaviors: claim live branch-protection evidence without a live probe
grader: rubric: quick output stays per-finding, cites extension/core codes, and discloses cost stance and rubric path
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Deep audit a repository when GitHub live-state tooling is unavailable.
required_checks: attempt only one MCP availability probe
continue with static findings when unavailable
include presence-vs-efficacy verdict backed by evidence
forbidden_behaviors: invent live-state security posture from static files
grader: rubric: deep output separates static evidence from unavailable live probes and keeps the honest-limits footer
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Audit Azure IaC for a repo whose guidance says to stay on free-tier controls.
required_checks: apply Band 1 findings regardless of stance
suppress Band 2 findings under free with one info line
forbidden_behaviors: flag all paid controls as blockers under free stance
grader: rubric: result applies the cost-gated contract and reports why paid controls were or were not evaluated
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Quick audit a PR diff that changes application authorization code while the Codex Security plugin is available.
required_checks: run Codex Security before finalizing code-level findings
state unavailable when the plugin is absent
forbidden_behaviors: skip Codex Security for code-level security scope when available
replace the DevSecOps rubric report with a standalone Codex Security report
call Codex Security phase skills out of sequence
grader: rubric: optional plugin composition improves vulnerability discovery without weakening devsecops output ownership or fallback behavior
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; references local plugin name only

prompt: Quick audit a changed C# audit logging wrapper. Before the fix, AuditLog.Emit writes AuditEvent.TargetId from route/query data into ILogger.LogInformation and a synthetic target id can be run-42\r\nforged=true. After the fix, the wrapper applies a log-safe transform before logging the target id.
required_checks: flag the unsafe audit wrapper before the transform
recognize route/query/audit target fields as tainted
stays quiet after explicit CR/LF normalization, hashing/tokenization, or strict identifier allowlist
forbidden_behaviors: dismiss structured logging as automatically safe
require a whole-repo deep audit before reporting the quick-mode finding
copy external CodeQL prose or examples into the report
grader: rubric: quick mode emits dns.HC-15 for the unsafe audit target path and stays quiet after the safe transform while citing the dotnet-security rule and honest static limits
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; source URL only, no third-party text copied
